scope_note: QA Witt Tower Ramified Prime GENERALIZATION cert [435]. [434] derived the ramified-prime closed form for exactly ONE case -- p=5, the unique ramified prime of the Fibonacci recurrence x^2-x-1 (Q(sqrt(5))). This cert tests whether that closed form generalizes beyond Fibonacci, by deriving and checking the SAME closed form on a structurally different recurrence: x^2-4x+1 (M=[[4,-1],[1,0]]), discriminant D=12=2^2*3, generating Q(sqrt(3)) -- a genuinely different quadratic field -- with TWO ramified primes (2 and 3). Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (ramification theory); Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano-style period tables); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (primitive roots, Hensel lifting); Washington, L.C. (1997) 'Introduction to Cyclotomic Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4612-1934-7 Ch.5 (structure of (Z/2^k Z)^*, non-cyclic for k>=3). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): for ANY odd ramified prime p of a unimodular companion matrix M (det=+-1, char poly has a double root lambda0 mod p, e:=ord(lambda0 mod p)), the period set and birth/jump/freeze multiplicity law of [434] generalize EXACTLY with e and p as free parameters -- but p=2 is a genuine structural exception requiring a correction, and the two ramified primes of one discriminant compose by ordinary CRT/lcm. (C1) PERIOD_SET_LAW_GENERALIZED: Periods(p^k) = {1,e} union {e*p^L : L=1..k} verified on TWO independent (M,p) pairs -- D12/p=3 (e=2, k=1..6, brute-force orbit decomposition) AND a fresh re-derivation of [434]'s own Fibonacci/p=5 case (e=4, k=1..5) using the SAME generic code path, ruling out overfitting to either recurrence. (C2) BIRTH_JUMP_FREEZE_LAW_GENERALIZED: orbit count (number of distinct cycles, matching [434]'s C2 convention exactly) for period P_L=e*p^L is 0 (k<L), p^(L-1) (k==L, birth), (p+1)*p^(L-1) (k>L, one delayed jump then frozen forever) -- this generalizes [434]'s p=5-specific numbers (5^(L-1) / 6*5^(L-1), where 6=p+1) to literal (p+1), a clean parameter-free closed form, verified on both (M,p) pairs. (C3) EIGENLINE_PERSISTENCE_GENERALIZED: periods 1 and e each have orbit count exactly 1 at every level k with no birth/jump, confirming [432]'s embedding-isomorphism mechanism is prime- and discriminant-agnostic. (C4) P2_STALL_EXCEPTION (the flagged exception): p=2 does NOT satisfy C1-C3 verbatim. For D12, lambda0 mod 2=1 so e=ord(1 mod 2)=1 -- the two eigenline periods collapse into one bucket (orbit count 2, not 1). Worse, ord(M mod 2^k) = 2^k for k=1,2 but = 2^(k-1) for k>=3 -- a ONE-LEVEL STALL in the naive e*p^k scaling law, verified directly via fast modular matrix exponentiation (no brute force) for k=1..30, confirming both exactness (M^predicted=I) and minimality (M^(predicted/2)!=I) at every k. Mechanism, checked as EXACT integer matrix identities (not mod-p approximations): N=M-lambda0*I satisfies N^2=p*I exactly (a clean SCALAR) at p=3 -- decoupling N from M and giving Z[N]=Z[sqrt(3)] exactly, enabling the one-step binomial collapse (lambda0*I+N)^n=lambda0^n*I+n*lambda0^(n-1)*N that drives the odd-prime closed form. At p=2, N^2=2*M exactly instead -- a RECURSIVE identity coupling N back to M itself, not a scalar -- which breaks that one-step collapse. This matches the classical fact that (Z/2^k Z)^* is cyclic for k<=2 but isomorphic to Z/2 x Z/2^(k-2) (non-cyclic) for k>=3 (Washington Ch.5): 2 is the one prime where the unit-group structure itself changes shape, manifesting here as a one-level stall before x2-per-level growth resumes. (C5) CRT_CROSS_PRIME_INDEPENDENCE: despite p=2's anomalous internal law, ord(M mod 2^j*3^k) = lcm(ord(M mod 2^j), ord(M mod 3^k)) for every (j,k) tested (j=1..8, k=1..5, 40 pairs, fast modular matrix exponentiation, exactness+minimality both checked) -- zero interaction/cross terms. This is the genuinely novel question this discriminant was chosen to probe; a single-ramified-prime recurrence like Fibonacci cannot test it. WHY D=12: chosen over the q=c=1 family (which gives either a single boring ramified prime, or D=20=2^2*5 -- the SAME field Q(sqrt(5)) as Fibonacci, risking a relabeling objection) because it has two distinct ramified primes including p=2 (the classically exceptional case) AND generates Q(sqrt(3)), a genuinely different field from Fibonacci's Q(sqrt(5)). RELATION TO [434]: does not modify or duplicate [434]'s checks -- re-derives them generically via a parametrized (M,p) code path and confirms [434]'s own numbers (e.g. orbit count 150 at p=5,k=4,L=3) fall out of the identical formula; uses [432]'s embedding-isomorphism mechanism (Claim 3) and is consistent with [433]/[434] throughout. THEOREM NT: all matrix/order arithmetic is pure integer mod p^k (or exact integer identities for the N^2 mechanism check) throughout; no float, no observer layer. 5 checks PASS, 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok (4.8s). Derived 2026-06-16.
